text: Map Aimparency to an automaton-style local autonomy runtime
description: Define how Aimparency's local service, graph, watchdog sessions, and .bowman state should map to a continuously running think/act/observe loop with heartbeat, identity, auditability, and local-first constraints. Use Conway Research's automaton as inspiration, but keep Aimparency local-first and git-centric rather than cloud-dependent.

reflections.context: Turn the broad autonomy/ASI direction into a concrete local-first runtime architecture for Aimparency, informed by automaton-style systems but grounded in Aimparency's graph and .bowman model.
reflections.outcome: Created a written autonomy runtime note that maps aims/phases/reflections/watchdog sessions onto a durable think-act-observe loop with heartbeat, policy, identity, and audit concepts. It also identifies what Aimparency should not copy directly from cloud-first autonomy systems.
reflections.effectiveness: This was effective because it reduced a vague ambition into a system boundary that can drive code changes. It gives a stable frame for future implementation aims without forcing premature cloud/economic assumptions.
reflections.lesson: For autonomy work, architecture notes are valuable when they sharply define what becomes durable state, what remains a worker concern, and where operator control lives.
reflections.pattern: This follows the same pattern as release work: convert broad product direction into explicit local-first architecture before making deeper implementation commitments.

status.comment: Documented the automaton-style local autonomy runtime mapping in meta/autonomy-runtime.md, including hypotheses, runtime split, and near-term implementation stages.
